sections.title: The passive (continuous, perfect, past perfect, be going to, modals)

content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: be in the correct tense + past participle

content.subtitle: Tense transformations (active → passive)
rows: The chef is preparing the desserts.
The desserts **are being prepared** by the chef.
The school has organised the event.
The event **has been organised** by the school.
The workers were repairing the bridge.
The bridge **was being repaired** by the workers.
The police had closed the road.
The road **had been closed** by the police.
They are going to build a new stadium.
A new stadium **is going to be built**.
We can solve the problem easily.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **by** to introduce the person or thing that performs the action: The mural was painted **by** local artists.
Use **with** to introduce the tool or instrument used: The bread was cut **with** a sharp knife.
Leave out **by** or **with** when the agent or instrument is unknown or not relevant: The injured player was taken to hospital immediately.

sections.title: A: Change into the passive
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in the passive.

items.sentence: The manager is checking the documents.
items.answer: The documents are being checked by the manager.

items.sentence: The students have decorated the classroom.
items.answer: The classroom has been decorated by the students.

items.sentence: The engineers had completed the tunnel before winter.
items.answer: The tunnel had been completed by the engineers before winter.

items.sentence: They might postpone the meeting.
We can organise the event online.
items.answer: The event can be organised online.

sections.title: B: Complete with the correct passive form
sections.instructions: Use the verb in brackets to complete each sentence in the passive.

items.sentence: The sports hall ...... (decorate) at the moment.
All the tickets ...... (reserve) already.
The computers ...... (install) when we arrived.
The decision ...... (approve) before the meeting started.
A new shopping centre ...... (open) next year.
Food ...... (not eat) in the science lab.

sections.title: C: by or with
sections.instructions: Write by, with, or - (no word needed).

items.sentence: The article was written ...... a famous journalist.
The glass was cleaned ...... a special cloth.
My wallet was stolen ...... last night.
The vegetables were chopped ...... a large knife.
The winning design was chosen ...... the judges.

sections.title: D: Mixed passive forms
sections.instructions: Choose the correct passive form for each sentence.

items.sentence: The files ...... before the manager arrived.
The new airport ...... at the moment.
The visitors ...... to wait outside the building.
The meal ...... when we entered the restaurant.
All the work ...... by next Friday.
The package ...... at the office since Monday.
